operating_ranges.evidence: Kanthal® AF Maximum Temperatures: RAC: 1,300°C (2,370°F), ROB: 1,300°C (2,370°F)
Kanthal® A-1 Maximum Temperatures: RAC: 1,300°C (2,370°F), ROB: 1,300°C (2,370°F)
Kanthal® APM Maximum Temperatures: FIBROTHAL® MEANDER SYSTEM II: 1,350°C (2,460°F), FIBROTHAL® MEANDER SYSTEM III: 1,350°C (2,460°F)
Hot resistance is approximately 4% higher, equal to 9.8 Ω

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Hot resistance is approximately 4% higher, equal to 9.8 Ω
iron oxide scale can damage Kanthal® heating elements, use a protective cover
Attacks all types of resistance alloys

fault_conditions.name: heating_element_failure
fault_conditions.description: Heating element must be replaced when out of order
fault_conditions.evidence: Heating element must be replaced when out of order

fault_conditions.name: electrical_bridge_formation
fault_conditions.description: Vapours form condensates from salts or oxides creating electrical bridges
fault_conditions.evidence: Vapours must not form condensates from salts or oxides, otherwise electrical bridges will be formed

fault_conditions.name: lead_fracture
fault_conditions.description: Stresses can fracture the lead at the element weld
fault_conditions.evidence: This action is necessary to preclude stresses can fracture the lead at the element weld

maintenance_actions.name: heating_element_replacement
maintenance_actions.evidence: Heating element must be replaced when out of order

maintenance_actions.name: burn_in_and_oxidation
maintenance_actions.evidence: After adjustment, follow procedure described in item 0 (Burn-in and Oxidation of an Heating element)

maintenance_actions.name: heating_element_adjustment
maintenance_actions.evidence: Adjustment of the heating element to reach correct position may be done by the heating element support

assumptions.statement: Temperature factor influencing heating resistance is negligible for Kanthal A-1
assumptions.reason: Manual states maximum resistance increase is only 4%

assumptions.statement: Power terminals require careful handling during maintenance
assumptions.reason: Manual emphasizes using two box-end wrenches to prevent stress fractures
